creator: tobik@freebsd.org
text:
Hi,

guile3-3.0.10 is broken (crash) with regards to any calls to exec* or
system* and it's probably the same bug as this one here.  To reproduce
try running: guile3 -e '(system* "ls")'

Compiling Guile from git af96820e072d18c49ac03e80c6f3466d568dc77d
yields the same result.  Backtrace when running (system* "ls") from
the REPL:

#0  0x000000082831c5c5 in posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np () from /lib/libc.so.7
#1  0x00000008214dc6c5 in do_spawn (exec_file=exec_file@entry=0x245d59808040 "ls", exec_argv=exec_argv@entry=0x83e9c7310, exec_env=exec_env@entry=0x245d5989c000, in=in@entry=14,
    out=out@entry=1, err=err@entry=2, spawnp=1) at posix.c:1415
#2  0x00000008214dcc6a in piped_process (pid=pid@entry=0x820f19e28, prog=<optimized out>, args=<optimized out>, from=from@entry=0x904, to=to@entry=0x904) at posix.c:1588
#3  0x00000008214dd108 in scm_system_star (args=<optimized out>) at posix.c:1689
#4  0x00000008214c54cd in vm_debug_engine (thread=0x82bb93d80) at /home/tobias/guile/libguile/vm-engine.c:972
#5  0x00000008214cbdbc in scm_call_n (proc=0x82d07e030, argv=argv@entry=0x820f19f88, nargs=nargs@entry=1) at vm.c:1624
#6  0x0000000821442249 in scm_primitive_eval (exp=<optimized out>, exp@entry=0x82caf19c0) at eval.c:671
#7  0x00000008214422a4 in scm_eval (exp=0x82caf19c0, module_or_state=module_or_state@entry=0x82cae5c80) at eval.c:705
#8  0x00000008214a16e9 in scm_shell (argc=1, argv=0x820f1a518) at script.c:357
#9  0x000000082145b785 in invoke_main_func (body_data=0x820f1a410) at init.c:312
#10 0x000000082143b5b2 in c_body (d=0x820f1a340) at continuations.c:430
#11 0x00000008214c967b in vm_regular_engine (thread=0x82bb93d80) at /home/tobias/guile/libguile/vm-engine.c:972
#12 0x00000008214cbdbc in scm_call_n (proc=0x82ca785a0, argv=argv@entry=0x820f1a150, nargs=nargs@entry=2) at vm.c:1624
#13 0x000000082144113e in scm_call_2 (proc=<optimized out>, arg1=<optimized out>, arg2=<optimized out>) at eval.c:503
#14 0x0000000821442961 in scm_c_with_exception_handler (type=type@entry=0x404, handler=handler@entry=0x8214c07d0 <catch_post_unwind_handler>, handler_data=handler_data@entry=0x820f1a260,
    thunk=thunk@entry=0x8214c0920 <catch_body>, thunk_data=thunk_data@entry=0x820f1a260) at exceptions.c:170
#15 0x00000008214c0b4a in scm_c_catch (tag=tag@entry=0x404, body=body@entry=0x82143b5a0 <c_body>, body_data=body_data@entry=0x820f1a340, handler=handler@entry=0x82143b870 <c_handler>,
    handler_data=handler_data@entry=0x820f1a340, pre_unwind_handler=pre_unwind_handler@entry=0x82143b6a0 <pre_unwind_handler>, pre_unwind_handler_data=0x82ca56820) at throw.c:168
#16 0x000000082143bb0a in scm_i_with_continuation_barrier (body=body@entry=0x82143b5a0 <c_body>, body_data=body_data@entry=0x820f1a340, handler=handler@entry=0x82143b870 <c_handler>,
    handler_data=handler_data@entry=0x820f1a340, pre_unwind_handler=pre_unwind_handler@entry=0x82143b6a0 <pre_unwind_handler>, pre_unwind_handler_data=0x82ca56820) at continuations.c:368
#17 0x000000082143bba9 in scm_c_with_continuation_barrier (func=<optimized out>, data=<optimized out>) at continuations.c:464
#18 0x00000008214bf4fc in with_guile (base=0x820f1a3a8, data=0x820f1a3e0) at threads.c:649
#19 0x0000000821d13f9b in GC_call_with_stack_base () from /usr/local/lib/libgc-threaded.so.1
#20 0x00000008214bf88c in scm_i_with_guile (dynamic_state=<optimized out>, data=0x820f1a3c0, func=0x82145b760 <invoke_main_func>) at threads.c:692
#21 scm_with_guile (func=func@entry=0x82145b760 <invoke_main_func>, data=data@entry=0x820f1a410) at threads.c:698
#22 0x000000082145b935 in scm_boot_guile (argc=argc@entry=1, argv=argv@entry=0x820f1a518, main_func=main_func@entry=0x4009f0 <inner_main>, closure=closure@entry=0x0) at init.c:295
#23 0x00000000004008aa in main (argc=1, argv=0x820f1a518) at guile.c:94

During the build there's this compiler warning:

posix.c: In function 'do_spawn':
posix.c:1415:45: warning: passing argument 1 of 'posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np' from incompatible pointer type [-Wincompatible-pointer-types]
 1415 |   posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np (&actions, 3);
      |                                             ^~~~~~~~
      |                                             |
      |                                             rpl_posix_spawn_file_actions_t *
In file included from ../lib/spawn.h:42,
                 from posix.c:37:
/usr/include/spawn.h:94:46: note: expected 'struct __posix_spawn_file_actions **' but argument is of type 'rpl_posix_spawn_file_actions_t *'
   94 | int posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np(posix_spawn_file_actions_t *,
      |                                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

A couple of things:

1. Guile's build picks the replacement posix_spawn(3) implementation
   instead of using the native FreeBSD implementation.
2. Guile's rpl_posix_spawn*() lacks an implementation of
   posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np()
3. posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np() is picked up from
   FreeBSD's own implementation.
4. rpl_posix_spawn_*() and FreeBSD's posix_spawn_*() are obviously
   incompatible and mixing them cannot work.  Hence the build warning
   and runtime crash.

It should be investigated why FreeBSD's native posix_spawn(3) is not
enough for Guile and why it insists on using rpl_posix_spawn*().  I
kind of doubt that this is actually true and that the configure script
has a bug and detects it wrong.

For the time being my suggestion would be to disable detection of
posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np(). This can be done now as a
workaround to have a working guile3 again:

CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	ac_cv_func_posix_spawn_file_actions_addclosefrom_np=no

Thanks!
